---
title:    "🎨 Built something on ReaLM-Retrieve? Drop it here."
category: show-and-tell
pin:      true
lock:     false
---

If you've used **ReaLM-Retrieve** for something — a paper, a blog post, a
demo, an internal tool, a course project — we'd love to see it. Reply to
this thread (or open a new one) with a short post:

```markdown
**What you built**
One-line summary.

**Why it's interesting**
Two or three sentences. The "huh, that's neat" angle.

**Where to find it**
Repo / paper / blog post / Twitter thread URL.

**What you wished was easier**
Optional but very welcome — it shapes the roadmap.
```

## We feature good ones

- The most interesting projects get linked from the **README's "Used by"
  section** (with your permission).
- Reproducible benchmarks on new datasets feed into a wiki page —
  [tracker issue TBD], reaction 👍 to vote.
- Substantive research extensions can become **co-authorships on a
  follow-up workshop paper**. See [#27 multilingual support](https://github.com/bettyguo/realm-retrieve/issues/27)
  for the live example.

## Norms

- **Attribute clearly.** If you've forked or modified the code, link the
  upstream commit you started from.
- **Cite the paper.** BibTeX is [here](https://github.com/bettyguo/realm-retrieve/wiki/Citation).
- **No self-promotion overload.** One announcement thread per project; new
  features get replies, not new threads.
- **OK to share private work.** "I can't share the full code, but here's
  what I learned" posts are valuable too.

Looking forward to seeing what you make.
